name: Statement Ingestion
description: RECOMMENDED APPROACH A — STAGED LOCAL ADAPTER PIPELINE. Treat ingestion as a provenance-and-commit protocol, not as a universal document parser. A versioned source adapter for each evidenced bank format produces an immutable candidate batch and reconciliation manifest without mutating LEDGER. INGEST validates account mapping, exact amounts, currency, source dates, source references, source fingerprint and row identity, then exposes an explicit preview. Commit invokes only the discoverable versioned LEDGER contract with stable idempotency identities and emits a resumable receipt covering every candidate. Start with the smallest real formats needed by the three accounts; an adapter seam permits later formats without building a generic extraction platform. Complexity M for the two evidenced text-native PDF layouts; it exceeds the budget if it turns into generic OCR. Root-problem completeness 9/10. It builds directly on FR-LEDGER-CONTRACT-DISCOVERY, FR-LEDGER-STRUCTURED-INVOCATION, FR-LEDGER-TRANSACTION-RECORDING, and FR-LEDGER-IDEMPOTENT-WRITES. Biggest risk: a parser can produce financially plausible but semantically wrong rows. Stress test: if representative fixtures cannot reconcile every source row and required field deterministically, that format remains unsupported and the do-less path wins. ALTERNATIVE B — DIRECT STREAMING IMPORT. Parse a file and immediately invoke LEDGER once per row, relying on idempotency for retry. Complexity M and root completeness 6/10. It reduces staging work, but parsing errors and partial commits are visible only after durable mutation, review is weak, and recovery semantics become harder to explain. Rejected because the safety loss attacks the root problem. Biggest risk: a partially committed or misparsed batch looks complete. ALTERNATIVE C — DO LESS WITH NORMALIZED CSV. Require bank-provided CSV or an owner-prepared canonical CSV and implement only validation plus commit. Complexity L, root completeness 5/10, and strongly within budget. This is an honest first-format or fallback when all initial accounts export reliable machine-readable data; it loses direct PDF and Excel support and preserves manual conversion work. Biggest risk: manual normalization recreates the omissions and semantic drift INGEST exists to prevent. SELECTION. Choose Approach A, but constrain it to evidenced adapters and allow Approach C to replace any format that cannot be parsed locally and reconciled deterministically. The non-obvious inversion is that parsers are disposable boundary adapters; the durable product is the manifest, provenance, preview, idempotency, and receipt contract. SCOPE SIGNALS AND ROUTING. Signals present: sensitive financial-data security, external bank-file formats, a cross-system flow into LEDGER, a likely import model exceeding five entities, and significant unknowns around PDF extraction, locale, sign, date, duplicate identity, reconciliation, and source retention. Five signals plus security and major unknowns route next to /lex:discovery INGEST. A requirement for a new cross-module LEDGER batch contract or a project-wide source-retention policy should be considered for /lex:adr after discovery evidence; this brainstorm does not decide either.

DISCOVERY 0.3 — FIXTURE EVIDENCE. A privacy-safe throwaway spike covered all three private local statements across both institutions: eight text-native PDF pages, two detectable layouts, ZAR, one asset/liability mix, deterministic dates, and exact opening-plus-movement-to-closing reconciliation. One layout has permission encryption but remains text-extractable without an opening password. The evidence also found zero-amount non-movement source records, no explicit transaction IDs, and one duplicate date-description-amount tuple. OQ-INGEST-1, 2, 3, and 5 are resolved; OQ-INGEST-4 remains open because cross-file overlap and corrected-statement identity are not proven.

ACTORS AND CURRENT STATE. P1 Personal Finance Owner is reused as the sole human actor. The AI Agent Host is a supporting automated client, not a persona. Today P1 downloads separate statements, manually reads or converts them, transcribes selected facts, infers sign/date/account meaning, removes suspected duplicates or transfers, and recalculates totals. The pain is slow recurring work, incomplete coverage, unreproducible conversion choices, weak duplicate confidence, and no durable proof that every source transaction reached LEDGER.

DESIRED WORKFLOWS AND GAPS. UC-INGEST-001 passively detects, extracts, validates, reconciles, and previews one local statement without mutation. UC-INGEST-002 reviews the exact immutable manifest and requires explicit approval without editing source financial facts. UC-INGEST-003 commits candidates through public idempotent LEDGER writes and resumes interruptions from a durable receipt. UC-INGEST-004 distinguishes exact replay/duplicates from blocking overlap conflicts. UC-INGEST-005 reports and cleans up failed or abandoned ingestion safely. UC-INGEST-006 lets P1 or an AI Agent Host discover and invoke the complete machine contract. CLASSIFY is an explicit separate post-commit command, not an INGEST side effect.

PRE-MORTEM. Six months after launch, failure is most likely if (1) a bank layout drifts and a plausible but wrong amount, sign, date, account, or row mapping reaches LEDGER — RISK-INGEST-001/002; (2) replay, overlap, corrected statements, or a crash creates duplicates or an unknowable partial batch — RISK-INGEST-003/005; or (3) source, temporary, manifest, or receipt data leaks or malformed active content exhausts the host — RISK-INGEST-004/007. These risks drive the deep areas and the fixture, frozen-manifest, exact-only duplicate, passive-parsing, owner-only, and resume rules.
DOMAIN AREA DISPOSITIONS.
- auth/authz — CHECK: the sole-owner local process trusts the invoking OS identity; any daemon, remote client, UI, or multi-user mode reopens authentication/authorization and is outside INGEST.

- data model and persistence — DEEP: immutable manifest revisions, candidate outcomes, durable receipts, retention, exact replay, overlap conflicts, idempotency, and crash resume are central to G1/G3/G4/G5 and RISK-INGEST-003/005.
- UI and navigation — N/A: NG8 assigns screens and five-state presentation to CHANNELS; INGEST exposes a structured CLI contract only.
- integrations — DEEP: owner-supplied statement data, AI Agent Host invocation, public LEDGER writes, version compatibility, and the explicit post-commit CLASSIFY boundary determine the workflow.
- background and async — N/A: NG7 forbids watchers, schedulers, and a required daemon; every import is explicitly invoked.

- reporting and analytics — CHECK: INGEST reports extraction/reconciliation evidence and terminal counts only; budgets, trends, narrative insight, and ongoing account reconciliation remain BUDGET/INSIGHTS or NG6.
- notifications — N/A: no INGEST goal sends notifications; any delivery surface belongs to CHANNELS.

- configuration and admin — CHECK: supported adapters, safe limits, retained batch status, abandon, and cleanup are discoverable operations; no bank credential, live connector, or arbitrary parser configuration is introduced.
- multi-tenancy and isolation — N/A: the accepted product boundary is one Personal Finance Owner under one OS identity; multi-person or hosted isolation is outside scope.

- source format and financial semantics — DEEP: exact record boundaries, locale, account mapping, amount sign, dates, source identity, passive parsing, and source controls determine whether G1/G2/G3/G6 are trustworthy.

- local security and privacy — DEEP: sensitive source/temp/manifest/receipt artifacts, passive bounded processing, owner-only permissions, metadata-only diagnostics, source preservation, and crash cleanup drive C3/C11/C12 and RISK-INGEST-004/007.

INTEGRATED DOMAIN RULES. A supported Format Variant is fixture-proven, explicitly detectable, passive, bounded, and fail-closed; extension matching is insufficient. P1 supplies one active canonical account identity and statement metadata validates it. Every source record is accounted for explicitly. A transaction-bearing record must be accepted, proven an Exact Duplicate, or blocked; any rejected or ambiguous transaction-bearing record keeps the batch uncommittable, while non-transaction records may be excluded with a stable reason. Source-derived financial facts cannot be edited in INGEST. Preview freezes a Reviewed Manifest Revision; any decision change creates a new preview. Commit never rereads the source and may progress candidate by candidate only with stable LEDGER identities and a durable outcome before continuation. Exact Replay is safe; only identity-grade matches are automatic duplicates; similarity produces an Overlap Conflict. Resume reuses the same manifest, request, versions, and identities. The source stays caller-owned and unchanged.

SECURITY AND COMPLIANCE. Selective threat analysis covers disclosure, source/manifest tampering, outcome repudiation, and parser denial/resource exhaustion. Owner-only storage, no payload logging or process arguments, frozen fingerprints/manifests, attributable receipts, passive content handling, safe resource bounds, source preservation, and deterministic cleanup address them. Spoofing/elevation are bounded by the accepted OS-identity trust boundary. Analogous LEDGER discovery found the current purely personal/household local scope outside POPIA processing obligations; cloud parsing, hosting, multi-person access, or third-party processing requires reassessment and currently violates a boundary or kill criterion.

FEASIBILITY. G1 known/high once a variant is qualified; G2 known/high through the accepted LEDGER transaction contract; G3 complex/high through full row accounting and immutable review; G4 known/medium-high through accepted LEDGER discovery/structured/idempotent contracts, with runtime conformance still unbuilt; G6 known/high for the two evidenced text-native PDF variants across all three initial accounts, while changed layouts and other formats remain unsupported; G5 remains medium because OQ-INGEST-4 lacks overlapping or corrected fixtures; G7 known/high by analogy to the validated LEDGER Agent Host contract. The PRD may harden only the two evidenced PDF variants and must keep overlap handling fail-closed.

ASSUMPTION BASELINE FOR PRD. A1 and A2 are validated by the three fixtures. A3 is invalidated: exact-file replay is deterministic, but explicit cross-statement row identity is absent and tuple-only matching collides. A4 supplies a conservative minimal-retention default and remains owner-revisable. OQ-INGEST-6 through OQ-INGEST-10 are resolved to candidate-granular resume, minimal retention, explicit post-commit classification, explicit account selection, and stable safe diagnostic categories.

SCOPE NOTE. Discovery operationalizes existing goals through six use cases, six additional constraints, four assumptions, dependency gates, security risk RISK-INGEST-007, metrics, and precise terminology. It does not add live banking, category selection, analytics, generic OCR, private LEDGER access, account reconciliation, background automation, or UI.

KILL-CRITERIA STATUS. No criterion is triggered. All three initial PDF fixtures are machine-readable and reconcile exactly under two bounded adapters, so the initial PDF scope may proceed. OCR, XLSX, CSV, and other adapters remain unsupported until separately evidenced, and normalized CSV remains the do-less fallback for future unqualifiable formats. The selected protocol requires no cloud upload, bank credentials, custom cryptography, or generic OCR. The accepted LEDGER contract supplies the required provenance and idempotent per-candidate writes without private coupling. Fixture scope remains bounded to the initial accounts. The fixture spike recheck found no trigger; recheck before planning only for adapter-scope or dependency drift.
problem_statement: Manual entry cannot keep the three-account Financial Ledger complete, while the three real private PDF statements use two layouts and encode account class, amount sign, row dates, balances, and non-movement records differently. The 5 Whys chain is: manual entry is too slow for historical and recurring data; the files contain the needed scale; each layout encodes facts differently; ad hoc parsing can silently omit, duplicate, or misstate plausible rows; and every later classification, budget, and insight becomes untrustworthy when canonical facts are wrong. The root problem is the absence of a deterministic local statement-to-LEDGER import boundary that preserves source provenance, exposes ambiguity before mutation, reconciles every supported statement, and makes retries duplicate-safe. The desired journey is agent-first: the owner downloads a statement, an agent discovers INGEST operations, previews candidates and reconciliation evidence for an explicitly selected account, reviews an immutable manifest, explicitly commits accepted movements through public LEDGER commands, and receives a receipt proving every accepted, duplicate, excluded, blocked, and failed outcome.
why_now: All three initial private statements are now available and the fixture spike proves two bounded PDF adapters are feasible while exposing the credit-account, zero-amount-row, and duplicate-identity edge cases the PRD must make explicit before LEDGER implementation is compiled.

rationale: Every accepted statement row must become or match a canonical ledger transaction.
